name: Lying Cable Pullover
resistance_profile: Lying with a low pulley behind your head, the cable pulls hardest with the arms overhead and behind you — loading the lats (and the sternal chest) deep in the stretch; tension eases as the arms arc over the chest.
cues: Lie on a flat bench, low pulley behind your head
Arms nearly straight, arc up and over
Deep stretch overhead, don't flare the ribs
execution_steps: Set a low pulley behind the head of a flat bench, attach a straight or rope handle, and lie back holding it with arms nearly straight overhead.
Let the cable draw your arms back and overhead into a deep stretch across the lats and chest, keeping the ribs down and core braced.
Keeping the elbows locked at a soft, fixed angle, arc the handle over your chest toward your hips using the lats.
Control the return to the overhead stretch; the loaded stretch behind the head is the point of the exercise.
good_when: You want a lat isolation that loads the deep overhead stretch with constant cable tension.
You want to pre-exhaust or finish the lats (with some sternal-chest involvement) without loading the biceps or lower back.
You need light, joint-friendly loads for higher-rep work.
bad_when: You have limited time and need one movement to build overall back mass (pick a pulldown or row).
You want to move heavy loads for strength, since isolation caps the useful weight.
No flat bench is free — the standing straight-arm pulldown is the upright alternative.
common_errors: Bending the elbows to turn it into a pressdown
Flaring the ribs / overarching to reach further
Using the triceps to push the handle down
